title: Old Friends
desc: A chance encounter in Kiru City puts you on Maxxor's radar — for the right reasons.
chainDesc: A conversation about an old friend draws you into Maxxor's deepest wound.

steps.text:
Kiru City is busy today. OverWorld soldiers drill in the courtyard, Muges ferry scrolls between towers, and a handful of Chaotic players mill around hoping for scans. You're calibrating your scanner near the central market when you bump into an OverWorld creature — older, tired-looking, carrying a bundle of supplies.

He drops half of it. You help him pick it up.

"Thanks, human. Most of your kind just point those devices at me and run." He eyes your scanner. "You here to scan someone important?"

You end up talking. He mentions the old days — before the M'arrillian invasion, before everything got complicated. He brings up Raznus.

options.label: "Raznus was a good creature. What happened to him wasn't his fault."
"I've read about Raznus. He taught Maxxor something important when they were young."
Try to scan the creature while he's talking

steps.text:
The creature looks at you like you've said something he hasn't heard in a long time.

He clasps your hand. "It's good to hear someone remember him right."

He leaves. You go back to your scanner.

Twenty minutes later, a shadow falls over you. You look up. And up.

Maxxor.

"Gadrel told me what you said about Raznus." He's quiet for a moment.

"I need someone I can trust with something sensitive. Not an OverWorld soldier — I need someone like yourself, someone who can be here one moment and there the next."

He leans in.

"My scouts have spotted Danian operatives at Ravanaugh Ridge. Not traders. Not diplomats. They're running the same patterns we saw before the first infections — scouting positions, mapping patrol routes, testing perimeters. I think they're planning to weaponize the parasite again."

His jaw tightens.

"I have to know now"

He looks at you.

"Go to Ravanaugh Ridge. Use your scanner. Get me proof — or tell me I'm paranoid. Either way, I'll owe you."
